refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It does not include multiple obligations or implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets
Use a redundant communication path strategy to ensure packet loss stays below the threshold
Incorporate a buffering and smoothing mechanism at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets

potential_sources: Tight integration between monitoring and control functions could create coupling if not modularized
Dependency on external network conditions may introduce indirect coupling
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates monitoring from control actions, supporting modularity. It focuses on dynamic adjustment rather than fixed hardware, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be monitored accurately and in real-time
Risk that dynamic adjustments may not fully compensate for sudden network degradations
Assumes operational policies allow for adaptive communication parameter changes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of network conditions and define interfaces between monitoring and control modules to ensure modular implementation and reduce coupling risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses network-related functionalities including monitoring and control of network parameters to ensure quality of service.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network performance data and send monitoring reports.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to adjust transmission parameters and receive network status updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as network quality monitoring and control logically belong here; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module is focused on physical vehicle functions; network control would be diffuse and poorly contained.
Teleoperation depends on network quality but does not own network control; partial containment but some diffusion.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts, not network control; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the network quality monitoring and control DP, as it centralizes network-related functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Teleoperation for network status feedback may cause minor coupling.
If network control logic is tightly coupled with vehicle hardware, some diffusion to Vehicle module may occur.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet the packet loss targets defined by the customer. This directly supports the teleoperation requirement with the specified communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Teleoperation for network status feedback may cause minor coupling.
If network control logic is tightly coupled with vehicle hardware, some diffusion to Vehicle module may occur.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion risk. It is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP, centralizing network-related functions and minimizing diffusion across other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and adjusting transmission parameters via the Network Link interface, ensuring packet loss remains within customer-defined limits.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well-justified, with interfaces defined for necessary data exchange and control, supporting traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document and manage the dependency on the Teleoperation module to minimize coupling and ensure clear interface contracts.
Assess and mitigate any coupling between network control logic and Vehicle hardware to prevent diffusion and maintain modularity.
Include verification steps in the worksite safety plan and operational policies to ensure packet loss targets are consistently met under agreed network conditions.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP logically addresses the FR, the IM containment is appropriate with minimal diffusion risk, and verification methods are feasible. Recommended revisions are minor and pertain to documentation and interface management rather than fundamental decomposition changes. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and stable architecture is paramount, and no further sub-FR decomposition is necessary at this stage.

revise.why: To explicitly document and manage dependencies and coupling risks identified, ensuring clear interface contracts and modularity to support safety and maintainability.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the FR for network quality control in teleoperation.
The NGHE Solution module is the best containment boundary for the DP with minimal diffusion risk.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Teleoperation module and Vehicle hardware that may affect modularity and maintainability.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss metrics remain within customer-defined limits under operational conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and dependency management between NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Vehicle modules.
